---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 02404ac0d778e9d0d4f461fc19f69ddf36384ad1
findings:
  - { id: judged-j-1, kind: judged, text: "ac-1's 'carried over byte-for-byte' is implemented as a full frontmatter re-render (align.RenderMarkdown/renderFrontmatter over a value-copied struct), not a byte splice: on a strict-decode-legal living report whose frontmatter formatting differs from the canonical renderer (block-style findings list, alternate quoting), every OTHER finding's line and the digest/integrity/judge_integrity lines are silently rewritten into canonical form. The spec's two operational verifier checks (ComputeDigest equality, VerifyIntegrity success) still hold since they are value-level, and align-generated reports are always canonical, so this only diverges on hand-authored-but-legal reports — the letter of 'byte-for-byte' is weaker than the implementation guarantees in that corner. (confidence 0.30)" }
  - { id: judged-j-4, kind: judged, text: "ADJ-52's j-3 fix guards only the --rationale input, not the finding-text input on the other side of the same rendered line: internal/align/judge.go:178 embeds the judge's jf.Text into Finding.Text with no control-character check, and Finding.Validate imposes none, so a judge emitting a JSON-escaped newline in a finding's text yields a legal, strict-decodable living report whose rendered bullet spans two body lines. replaceWholeLine (cmd/verdi/disposition.go) splits the body on newline and can then never match RenderFindingLine(oldFinding) — which itself contains the newline — as a whole line, so every disposition attempt on that finding exits 2 as an 'internal error': the same permanent, non-amendable no-sanctioned-escape-hatch dead end j-2 named, reachable through the align/judge pipeline's own documented inputs rather than the verb's. (confidence 0.25)" }
  - { id: judged-j-5, kind: judged, text: "When the target finding's rendered line cannot be located exactly once in the body (a body hand-drifted from its frontmatter, or produced by an older renderer whose line format differs), the verb exits 2 labeled 'internal error' (disposition.go's found-N path). dc-3's taxonomy classifies conditions about 'the report's own state' as verdicts (exit 1) and reserves 2 for environment/operational failures, and nothing operational has failed here — the report itself is in a state the verb refuses; the 'internal error' wording additionally misattributes an externally-authored report condition to a bug in the tool. ac-2 enumerates neither list as containing this case, so the choice is unrecorded rather than wrong, but the exit class and message diverge from the spec's own verdict-vs-operational intent. (confidence 0.18)" }
digest: sha256:5a0c82335ab0aa4ff45467281398c866346678c2b7dbe63f85242e016607e719
integrity: sha256:b7910dbb892bcf204becc060f98675f9d4fd8dc85d96f234805db86ecfa86435
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"j-1\",\"text\":\"ac-1's 'carried over byte-for-byte' is implemented as a full frontmatter re-render (align.RenderMarkdown/renderFrontmatter over a value-copied struct), not a byte splice: on a strict-decode-legal living report whose frontmatter formatting differs from the canonical renderer (block-style findings list, alternate quoting), every OTHER finding's line and the digest/integrity/judge_integrity lines are silently rewritten into canonical form. The spec's two operational verifier checks (ComputeDigest equality, VerifyIntegrity success) still hold since they are value-level, and align-generated reports are always canonical, so this only diverges on hand-authored-but-legal reports — the letter of 'byte-for-byte' is weaker than the implementation guarantees in that corner.\",\"confidence\":0.3},{\"id\":\"j-4\",\"text\":\"ADJ-52's j-3 fix guards only the --rationale input, not the finding-text input on the other side of the same rendered line: internal/align/judge.go:178 embeds the judge's jf.Text into Finding.Text with no control-character check, and Finding.Validate imposes none, so a judge emitting a JSON-escaped newline in a finding's text yields a legal, strict-decodable living report whose rendered bullet spans two body lines. replaceWholeLine (cmd/verdi/disposition.go) splits the body on newline and can then never match RenderFindingLine(oldFinding) — which itself contains the newline — as a whole line, so every disposition attempt on that finding exits 2 as an 'internal error': the same permanent, non-amendable no-sanctioned-escape-hatch dead end j-2 named, reachable through the align/judge pipeline's own documented inputs rather than the verb's.\",\"confidence\":0.25},{\"id\":\"j-5\",\"text\":\"When the target finding's rendered line cannot be located exactly once in the body (a body hand-drifted from its frontmatter, or produced by an older renderer whose line format differs), the verb exits 2 labeled 'internal error' (disposition.go's found-N path). dc-3's taxonomy classifies conditions about 'the report's own state' as verdicts (exit 1) and reserves 2 for environment/operational failures, and nothing operational has failed here — the report itself is in a state the verb refuses; the 'internal error' wording additionally misattributes an externally-authored report condition to a bug in the tool. ac-2 enumerates neither list as containing this case, so the choice is unrecorded rather than wrong, but the exit class and message diverge from the spec's own verdict-vs-operational intent.\",\"confidence\":0.18}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/disposition-verb@02404ac0d778e9d0d4f461fc19f69ddf36384ad1, spec/disposition-verb@d2ecf50f0e6f8a3163692abce22fe55de7adf3c2], digest: sha256:5a0c82335ab0aa4ff45467281398c866346678c2b7dbe63f85242e016607e719, integrity: sha256:b7910dbb892bcf204becc060f98675f9d4fd8dc85d96f234805db86ecfa86435 }

# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-j-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ac-1's 'carried over byte-for-byte' is implemented as a full frontmatter re-render (align.RenderMarkdown/renderFrontmatter over a value-copied struct), not a byte splice: on a strict-decode-legal living report whose frontmatter formatting differs from the canonical renderer (block-style findings list, alternate quoting), every OTHER finding's line and the digest/integrity/judge_integrity lines are silently rewritten into canonical form. The spec's two operational verifier checks (ComputeDigest equality, VerifyIntegrity success) still hold since they are value-level, and align-generated reports are always canonical, so this only diverges on hand-authored-but-legal reports — the letter of 'byte-for-byte' is weaker than the implementation guarantees in that corner. (confidence 0.30)
- **judged-j-4** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ADJ-52's j-3 fix guards only the --rationale input, not the finding-text input on the other side of the same rendered line: internal/align/judge.go:178 embeds the judge's jf.Text into Finding.Text with no control-character check, and Finding.Validate imposes none, so a judge emitting a JSON-escaped newline in a finding's text yields a legal, strict-decodable living report whose rendered bullet spans two body lines. replaceWholeLine (cmd/verdi/disposition.go) splits the body on newline and can then never match RenderFindingLine(oldFinding) — which itself contains the newline — as a whole line, so every disposition attempt on that finding exits 2 as an 'internal error': the same permanent, non-amendable no-sanctioned-escape-hatch dead end j-2 named, reachable through the align/judge pipeline's own documented inputs rather than the verb's. (confidence 0.25)
- **judged-j-5** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: When the target finding's rendered line cannot be located exactly once in the body (a body hand-drifted from its frontmatter, or produced by an older renderer whose line format differs), the verb exits 2 labeled 'internal error' (disposition.go's found-N path). dc-3's taxonomy classifies conditions about 'the report's own state' as verdicts (exit 1) and reserves 2 for environment/operational failures, and nothing operational has failed here — the report itself is in a state the verb refuses; the 'internal error' wording additionally misattributes an externally-authored report condition to a bug in the tool. ac-2 enumerates neither list as containing this case, so the choice is unrecorded rather than wrong, but the exit class and message diverge from the spec's own verdict-vs-operational intent. (confidence 0.18)
